meta.title: The Relay-Lock
meta.subtitle: A Prototypology for the 24h Healthcare Supply Chain
meta.studio: AR-302k Studio Huang — Sentient Cities, EPFL BA6
meta.author: Andrea Crespo
meta.description: 7-node healthcare chain along the Geneva–Villeneuve corridor. Each node is a 'Lock' managing a threshold between two incompatible states. The relay chain keeps the 24h city running — when one lock closes, the next must open.

concept.relay: System level — chain of 7 nodes along the corridor. Remove any node and the chain breaks.
concept.lock: Building level — architecture managing a threshold between two incompatible states, borrowed from canal lock logic.
concept.chamber: The architectural space where transition happens. Active during the 01:00–05:00 dead window when the corridor shuts down.
concept.key_insight: The occupant is stationary — the space transitions around them. Like a canal lock where the boat stays still while water levels change.
concept.dead_window: 00:30–05:00: zero weekday public transport on the entire 101km corridor. 4,600–6,300 healthcare workers stranded or car-dependent.

nodes.name: Geneva North Industrial Belt
nodes.description: ZIMEYSA industrial zone — 380+ hectares, 500+ companies. Where healthcare cargo meets the city. Swiss Post, pharma sorting, medical device manufacturing. Workers invisible despite being the backbone of the supply chain.
nodes.vertical_transport_analogy: Freight elevator — moves goods between incompatible floors. The cargo lock mediates between industrial logistics (trucks, pallets, cold chain) and urban fabric (streets, people, civic life).

nodes.name: Nyon Hospital + Genolier
nodes.description: GHOL Nyon in the valley (1,000 staff, 103k catchment), Clinique de Genolier on the hilltop (358 staff + 230 doctors). Last NStCM train to Genolier at 21:29 — after that, car-only. Two healthcare worlds separated by 400m of altitude.
nodes.vertical_transport_analogy: Funicular — connects two altitudes with a single mechanical system. The vertical connector links valley public hospital to hilltop private clinic, bridging the gap when the last train leaves at 21:29.

nodes.name: Morges Hospital Gap
nodes.description: EHC Morges — 2,000 staff, 150k catchment. The purest lock concept: the station exists, the hospital exists, but between 00:30 and 05:00 there is no connection. 300–400 night workers face a 4.5-hour void. The temporal lock bridges two moments in time.
nodes.vertical_transport_analogy: Airlock — a chamber that holds you while the environment changes. The temporal lock holds night-shift workers in a viable space while time transitions from 'last service' to 'first service'. The occupant waits; the city restarts around them.

nodes.name: Crissier-Bussigny Night Belt
nodes.chamber_program: Supply chain civic space + worker visibility
nodes.description: The densest healthcare logistics concentration on the corridor. PLEXUS-Santé (3,500 orders/day), CHUV food production (6,800 meals/day), Galexis pharma (100k packs/day), B. Braun (12M medical devices/year). Thousands of workers, zero civic presence. The visibility lock makes the invisible backbone legible.
nodes.vertical_transport_analogy: Service elevator — the one nobody sees, running behind the walls. The visibility lock reveals the hidden infrastructure that keeps hospitals running. It turns the service corridor into a civic space.

nodes.name: Lausanne CHUV
nodes.description: CHUV — 12,844 staff, 800k referral catchment. Best-connected hospital (M2 metro direct). But Lausanne's extreme topography creates an equity gradient: the M2 runs vertically, but the hospital's supply chain runs horizontally. The gradient dispatcher resolves the perpendicular conflict between the city's vertical movement and the corridor's horizontal flow.
nodes.vertical_transport_analogy: Elevator bank — multiple cars serving different floors simultaneously. The gradient dispatcher routes multiple supply chain flows across Lausanne's steep terrain. Unlike a single elevator, it must sort pharma, patients, staff, and emergency flows to different gradient levels.

nodes.name: Montreux–Glion
nodes.description: Montreux at lake level, Glion rehabilitation clinics at 700m altitude. The MOB and funicular connect two healthcare ecosystems — acute care below, long-term rehabilitation above. Families visiting rehab patients face a vertical journey the corridor doesn't acknowledge.
nodes.vertical_transport_analogy: Mountain railway — a mechanical system that makes altitude accessible. The altitude lock connects lake-level acute care with mountain rehabilitation, translating the vertical distance into an architectural threshold.

nodes.name: Rennaz Hospital Island
nodes.description: HRC Rennaz — 2,500 staff, 180k catchment. 2 km from Villeneuve station. 2.75× slower by public transport than car. A proposed monorail was never built. The newest major hospital on the corridor, yet the most disconnected from rail. PHEL pharmacy with global dispensing robot. The bridge lock spans the last-mile gap.
nodes.vertical_transport_analogy: Skybridge — a horizontal connector between two buildings that would otherwise require going down and back up. The bridge lock spans the 2km gap between rail corridor and hospital, a horizontal threshold that the system never resolved.

flows.name: Medication / Pharma
flows.description: Galexis, Alloga, Swiss Post Innight Medica. 100k packs/day from Ecublens hub.

flows.name: Food / Catering
flows.description: Hospital kitchens, CHUV CPA Bussigny, Eldora SA. 20,000+ meals/day across corridor.

flows.name: Staff Circulation
flows.description: 32,000+ hospital workers. 7,200 cross-border. 4,600–6,300 on night shifts.

flows.name: Patient Flows
flows.description: >10,000 CHUV transfers/year. 5 ERs for 101 km. Walk-in clinics close at night.

flows.name: Postal / Logistics
flows.description: Eclépens 24h (400+ staff), Daillens until 03:00. PLEXUS 3,500 orders/day.

flows.name: Blood / Lab Samples
flows.description: HUG CTS 14,500 bags/year. TIR 700+ mobile drives. SwissConnect couriers.

flows.name: Waste / Sterile
flows.description: 5,200+ tonnes HUG waste/year. TRIDEL, Cheneviers, SATOM incineration. CHUV sterilization.

flows.name: Emergency / Ambulance
flows.description: Two independent 144 dispatch systems. REGA 2 bases. Night crew reduction across corridor.

day.description: Full system — all 8 flow categories active, trains running, all nodes connected

evening.description: Reduced — last trains, shift changes, Innight Medica trucks loading, ambulance crew reduction begins

dead_window.description: All supply chains 100% road-dependent. Zero public transport. 4,600–6,300 workers stranded or driving.

dawn.description: System restart — first trains, dawn deliveries arrive, night shift ends at 07:00–07:15
